title: Trinity Eval Set
description: Personalized eval suite built from the user's empirical rejection signal. Each item is a (prompt, rejected_response, rejection_type) triple any candidate model can be scored against. Generated by `trinity-local eval-build`; lives at ~/.trinity/evals/<eval_id>.json. Replayable when new models ship: same eval_id, new model, new result file in ~/.trinity/evals/results/. Only Trinity can build this — no provider has cross-provider rejection signal.

eval_id.description: Content-addressed id (hash of source_ids). Same corpus state → same id, so re-running on unchanged corpus is idempotent and adding new rejections produces a new id (historical results stay pinned to the corpus state they scored against).

source.description: Which signal source produced the items. MVP supports `rejections` (turn-pair gap labels from ~/.trinity/me/rejections.jsonl). `cross_provider_pair` lands in a follow-up tick — same prompt asked of multiple providers.

by_rejection_type.description: Count of items per rejection_type, sorted descending. The dominant axis is the strongest benchmark signal for this user.

by_basin.description: Count of items per basin (topic cluster). Lets per-basin × rejection_type aggregation in scoring.

prompt.description: The original user prompt that elicited the rejected response. Falls back to `user_substitute` when the prompt index has churned and the prompt_id no longer resolves.

rejection_type.description: The four taste-terminal implicit rejection signal types. REFRAME: user substitutes a different frame. COMPRESSION: user wanted shorter. REDIRECT: user wanted a different topic entirely. SHARPENING: user wanted more precision on the same topic.

rejected_response.description: The assistant's response that triggered the user's rejection (model_quote in rejections.jsonl).

user_substitute.description: What the user actually wanted (their next-turn correction).

rubric_signal.description: Chairman-extracted why_signal — used by the scorer to know which axis to judge on.

basin_id.description: Topic cluster from topics.json. Enables per-basin × rejection_type benchmark aggregation.

source_id.description: The RejectionSignal.id (or cross-pair id when source=cross_provider_pair) this item was derived from.

prompt_id.description: PromptNode id when the original prompt was indexed. May be null when the prompt couldn't be located.

provider_of_rejected_response.description: Which provider produced the rejected response (claude/codex/gemini/...). Lets benchmarks report 'this model got rejected on N of M, the new model scored X' with provider attribution.
